Off Calendar Mar-14-2024 Set For Trial On Dec-02-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
Off Calendar Jan-29-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF OPERATORS GUILD TALENT DBA GUILD TALENT's APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's "application for right to attach order and order for issuance of writ of attachment" is denied. As plaintiff concedes, it had to establish its claim's probable validity in its moving papers. (CCP 481.190; Memo. 3:16-20.) However, its memorandum's validity "evidence" is limited to an allegation: "Defendants say that they have 'cash flow problems'" - without citation. (Id. at 5:1-5.) As detailed in the opposition, the dispute is far more complex than that. (Opp. 3:10-4:27.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk; V. Da Fonseca; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Dec-07-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Esther Oh, Esq. 415-926-7600 Kaufman Dolowich & Voluck LLP for Defendant.
No other Appearance.

RULING - Counsel does not contest the tentative ruling. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS CAPTAINHQ, INC. AND DEMETRIUS GRAY MOTION FOR ORDER SETTING ASIDE DEFAULTS.

Defendants' "motion for relief from defaults" is granted. Plaintiff sued defendants over recruiting services in June 2023. Defendants tried to resolve the matter without counsel. Plaintiff filed defaults on August 15 and September 1, 2023. Counsel retained, defendants filed this motion. It is the policy of California's courts to decide disputes on their merits and the motion is well within CCP 473(b)'s six-month window. (See Fasuyi v. Permatex, Inc. (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 681, 701.) Ethical lawyers warn before filing defaults. (Id.) Any mistake or neglect by defendants was excusable. (CCP 473(b).) Plaintiff may file a motion for "bond or deposit" if it wishes.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. , Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)
